'''Jastro III''' was a [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that was under the administration of a [[Alexan|governor]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. A [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] underground group operated on the planet, and the [[Governor/Legends|governor's]] [[Alexan's daughter|daughter]], who was in love with the Rebel [[Selas Ferr]], put the group in contact with "Alexan," a mysterious, influential individual who was secretly the governor himself in disguise. At one point, Alexan had Ferr's group take the governor's [[Gender/Legends|daughter]] away with them while helping them rescue two of their captured members. Alexan was subsequently [[Death/Legends|killed]] in a [[Lightfight|firefight]] with Imperial [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]], leading to Ferr and his companions learning his true identity.

==Description==
Jastro III was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that occupied the third [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position<ref name="HR" /> in the [[Jastro system/Legends|Jastro system]], a part of the [[Arrowhead]], or [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
{{RPGseed}}
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], Jastro III was overseen by a [[Alexan|planetary governor]] who had a [[Alexan's daughter|daughter]], with many personnel of the Imperial [[CompForce/Legends|CompForce]] organization being present on the planet. An Alliance underground group also operated there, with [[Selas Ferr]]—who was the lover of the [[Governor/Legends|governor's]] [[Gender/Legends|daughter]]—counting among its members. The woman, being unable to help Ferr due to the heavy Imperial presence, instead put him and his group into contact with a mysterious individual known as "Alexan," who held great influence in the governor's hierarchy—and was secretly the governor himself in disguise.<ref name="HR" />

At one point, Ferr's group carried out an abortive [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] operation, in the course of which two of the organization's men were captured. Ferr contacted Alexan for assistance, to which the secretive individual agreed on the condition that the resistance take the governor's daughter away with them along with their captured members. Alexan eventually delivered both the men and the woman to Ferr; however, Imperial [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] arrived before the hand-over was completed, leading to the outbreak of an intense [[Lightfight|firefight]]. During the skirmish, Alexan was [[Death/Legends|mortally]] wounded, and after Ferr and his companions reached safety, they discovered his true identity.<ref name="HR" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
Jastro III was mentioned in the [[1995]] sourcebook ''[[Heroes & Rogues]]'', authored by [[Rick D. Stuart|Rick Stuart]] and [[Paul Sudlow]] and published for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. In the book, the planet is mentioned in an example situation that served to illustrate the role influential but often-unavailable contacts may play in developing the backstory of the player characters. This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="HR" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Jastro [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Jastro III, in grid square M-10.<ref name="TEA" />
